name: libpysal (Spatial Weights and Spatial Econometrics Foundation)

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy-based; spatial weights construction can be slow for large datasets

agent_readiness.notes: Foundation for Python Spatial Analysis Library (PySAL). from libpysal.weights import Queen, Rook, KNN, Kernel, DistanceBand. w = Queen.from_dataframe(gdf). w.neighbors. w.weights. w.transform('r'). w.to_adjlist(). esda.Moran: from esda import Moran; mi = Moran(y, w); mi.I; mi.p_sim. spreg for spatial regression. Used as dependency by splot, esda, spreg, giddy, momepy. libpysal.io for spatial file I/O. Examples datasets: libpysal.examples.

gotchas: Usually used as dependency via esda/spreg, not directly
Spatial weights construction is slow for large polygons (>10K features)
No type annotations — poor IDE support
API is academic — spatial statistics background required
best_when: Spatial weights matrix construction for spatial statistics
Foundation for Moran's I, LISA, spatial regression (via esda/spreg)
When PySAL ecosystem tools (esda, spreg, splot) are being used
summary: libpysal is the foundation of the PySAL spatial analysis ecosystem. Queen/Rook/KNN.from_dataframe(gdf) for spatial weights. w.neighbors, w.transform('r'). Used as dependency by esda (Moran's I), spreg (spatial regression), splot. BSD-3. Essential for spatial statistics and spatial econometrics in Python. Usually imported through esda/spreg rather than directly. Spatial weights slow for large datasets.
